Sahara Desert – Camel Treks and Desert Camps

packages.package_name: Legendary Tour of Morocco, Spain & Portugal
packages.overview:
• Morocco: A land of contrasts, where the bustling souks and ancient medinas of cities like Marrakech and Fes meet the vastness of the Sahara Desert. Explore vibrant markets, historic palaces, and the stunning Atlas Mountains. Morocco is famous for its unique blend of Arab, Berber, and French influences.

  • Spain: Known for its lively culture, stunning architecture, and delicious cuisine, Spain offers everything from the artistic wonders of Barcelona and Madrid to the flamenco rhythms of Seville. Don't miss the beautiful beaches of Costa Brava and the historic allure of Andalusia.

  • Portugal: A country of stunning coastline, charming cities, and rich maritime history. Lisbon offers a mix of traditional and contemporary culture, while Porto is famous for its wine and scenic riverfront. The Algarve region boasts picturesque beaches, and the beautiful landscapes of Madeira and the Azores are perfect for nature lovers.

This morning, we’ll start with an included guided tour of Morocco’s largest city and economic center. We’ll stroll along the grand avenues, visit the beautiful Mohamed V Square with its fountain, Moorish buildings, and palm trees, and walk through the Arab League Park. We’ll also explore the little Medina. Our tour includes admission to the impressive Hassan II Mosque (the only mosque in Morocco open to non-Muslims), located by the sea and featuring a 210-meter-high minaret with laser lights pointing to Mecca. After the tour, you’ll have free time for lunch in the pleasant maritime area of La Corniche. After lunch, we’ll travel to El Jadida. Its citadel (Mazagan) is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, built by the Portuguese in the early sixteenth century. We’ll stroll through its picturesque streets, which retain many traces of the Portuguese era, such as the Manueline-style Church of the Assumption. We’ll also include a visit to the old sixteenth-century cistern. After exploring, our journey will continue to Safi, arriving in the late afternoon. Dinner will be included.

We’ll photograph the Castle of the Sea and its impressive fortress walls, stroll through the charming Medina, and visit the Hill of the Potters with its many shops.


Afterward, we’ll continue to Essaouira. Its walled Medina, facing the sea, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. This beautifully preserved city is a favorite among artists and painters. You’ll have free time for lunch and to wander through its narrow streets, whitewashed houses, and strong walls struck by the waves.

packages.overview:
• Hassan II Mosque – A majestic architectural masterpiece by the sea, boasting the world’s tallest minaret with laser beams pointing to Mecca.
  • El Jadida’s Citadel (Mazagan) – A UNESCO-listed Portuguese fortress with charming streets, ancient walls, and a Manueline-style church.
  • Essaouira Medina – A stunning coastal fortress with whitewashed houses, artistic alleys, and dramatic ocean waves crashing against its walls.
  • Jemaa el-Fnaa Square – The heart of Marrakech, alive with storytellers, snake charmers, vibrant souks, and mouthwatering street food.
  • Ouzoud Falls – Northern Africa’s tallest waterfalls, where you’ll enjoy a boat ride, scenic vistas, and playful wild macaques.
  • Fez Medina – Morocco’s cultural soul, an intricate labyrinth of souks, tanneries, and palaces transporting you to a bygone era.
  • Tangier – A captivating city where Africa and Europe meet, with bustling souks and panoramic views over the Strait of Gibraltar.
  • Seville Cathedral & Giralda – A towering Gothic wonder with breathtaking city views from its iconic bell tower.
  • Córdoba Mosque-Cathedral – A mesmerizing blend of Islamic and Christian artistry, with grand arches and intricate Moorish designs.
  • Consuegra Windmills – Legendary La Mancha landscapes featuring Don Quixote’s iconic windmills, standing tall against the Spanish plains.

This morning, we’ll start with an included guided tour of Morocco’s largest city and economic center. We’ll stroll along the grand avenues, visit the beautiful Mohamed V Square with its fountain, Moorish buildings, and palm trees, and walk through the Arab League Park. We’ll also explore the little Medina. Our tour includes admission to the impressive Hassan II Mosque (the only mosque in Morocco open to non-Muslims), located by the sea and featuring a 210-meter-high minaret with laser lights pointing to Mecca. After the tour, you’ll have free time for lunch in the pleasant maritime area of La Corniche. After lunch, we’ll travel to El Jadida. Its citadel (Mazagan) is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, built by the Portuguese in the early sixteenth century. We’ll stroll through its picturesque streets, which retain many traces of the Portuguese era, such as the Manueline-style Church of the Assumption. We’ll also include a visit to the old sixteenth-century cistern. After exploring, our journey will continue to Safi, arriving in the late afternoon. Dinner will be included.

With our guide, we’ll visit Safi, a city renowned for its ceramics, highly prized throughout Morocco for their quality and bright colors. We’ll photograph the Castle of the Sea and its impressive fortress walls, stroll through the charming Medina, and visit the Hill of the Potters with its many shops.


Afterward, we’ll continue to Essaouira. Its walled Medina, facing the sea, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. This beautifully preserved city is a favorite among artists and painters. You’ll have free time for lunch and to wander through its narrow streets, whitewashed houses, and strong walls struck by the waves.


After lunch, we’ll continue along the Atlantic coast, taking in the magnificent landscapes. We’ll make a brief stop in Taghazout, a picturesque small town known for its fishing and popular with surfers. Then, we’ll proceed to Agadir for accommodation, arriving in the evening. Dinner will be included.

packages.overview:
• Arrival in Morocco with airport transfer, hotel check-in, and welcome dinner.
  • Guided city tour of Casablanca including Hassan II Mosque; scenic drive to Marrakech with a carriage ride to Jemaa el-Fnaa.
  • Full-day guided city tour exploring Marrakech’s historic highlights and evening folklore dinner show (optional).
  • Journey through the Atlas Mountains with stops at Ait Benhaddou and Ouarzazate before reaching Boumalne Dades.
  • Explore Todgha Gorge and Ksar el Khorbat before arriving in the desert town of Erfoud for optional dune excursion.
  • Scenic drive through the Atlas Mountains with stops in Midelt, Azrou, and Bhalil, ending in Fez with dinner.
  • Guided city tour of Fez followed by a scenic drive north through the Rif valleys to Tangier.
  • Free time in Tangier, visit the seaside town of Asilah, then continue to Rabat for the night.
  • Morning city tour of Rabat and short drive to Casablanca for a relaxed final afternoon.
  • Breakfast and end of the journey with fond memories and Travelxploria assistance for departure.

At 08:00 a.m., we’ll depart from Marrakech and journey through the breathtaking Atlas Mountains, the natural divide between the city and the Sahara Desert. Along the way, we’ll stop at a charming mountain village. By 01:30 p.m., we’ll reach Ait Benhaddou, a UNESCO World Heritage site famed for its scenic mud-brick architecture featured in many films. After lunch and a stroll, we’ll continue to Ouarzazate at 03:30 p.m., another historic desert city. At 04:30 p.m., we travel onward through arid plains and oasis valleys, arriving in Boumalne Dades at 06:00 p.m. for dinner and overnight. Note: Route may change due to weather.

Along the way, admire oases, palm groves, and charming Berber villages. At 09:00 a.m., we’ll arrive at the majestic Todgha Gorge for a short exploration. By 10:45 a.m., we’ll stop at Ksar el Khorbat, a fortified desert village supported by the Europamundo Foundation. After departing at 11:45 a.m., we’ll reach Erfoud at 02:00 p.m. for lunch and relaxation. An optional sunset off-road excursion to Merzouga’s sand dunes is available with dinner.

We begin our journey from Erfoud at 08:00 a.m., crossing the majestic Atlas Mountains and scenic gorges, passing through a high plateau where nomadic shepherds dwell. At 11:30 a.m., we pause in Midelt for a brief walk. After departing at 12:00 p.m., we stop for an included lunch beside a serene mountain lake. The landscape then transforms into dense forests. At 03:15 p.m., we visit Azrou’s “House of Cedars” and observe native monkeys. After a 05:15 p.m. stop in the cave-dwelling town of Bhalil, we reach Fez by 06:45 p.m., admiring panoramic city views from the Marinid Tombs before dinner.

packages.overview:
• Tangier – Arrival in Tangier, hotel transfer, and dinner with evening briefing by Travelxploria representative.
  • Tangier – Asilah – Rabat – Morning free time in Tangier, visit charming Asilah, and arrive in the capital, Rabat, for dinner and overnight.
  • Rabat – Casablanca – El Jadida – Safi – Explore Rabat, tour Casablanca, visit historic El Jadida, and overnight in coastal Safi.
  • Safi – Essaouira – Taghazout – Agadir – Discover Safi ceramics, stroll through Essaouira’s medina, stop at Taghazout, and reach Agadir for dinner.
  • Agadir – Marrakech – Morning free at Agadir beach, then journey through the Atlas Mountains to lively Marrakech with evening Kalesa ride.
  • Marrakech – Guided tour of Marrakech’s highlights, including Bahia Palace and Jemaa el-Fnaa square; enjoy lunch and afternoon free.
  • Marrakech – Ouzoud – Fez – Scenic drive to Ouzoud Falls with lunch and boat ride, then continue through diverse landscapes to Fez.
  • Fez – Tangier – Explore Fez’s cultural treasures and medina, then journey north through the Rif to return to Tangier.
  • Tangier – Departure – After breakfast, end of tour with memories of a rich Moroccan journey; Travelxploria support until departure.​

packages.overview:
• ​Arrival in Marrakech, transfer to hotel, evening visit to Jemaa el-Fnaa square by caleche.
  •  Guided city tour of Marrakech including Medina, Bahia Palace, and free afternoon.
  • Travel across Atlas Mountains via Ait Benhaddou and Ouarzazate to Boumalne Dades.
  •  Explore the Valley of the Kasbahs, Todgha Gorge, Ksar el Khorbat, and relax in Erfoud.
  • Journey through Middle Atlas with stops in Midelt, Azrou, Bhalil, ending in Fez.
  •  Visit Fez Medina, Roman ruins at Volubilis, holy Moulay Idriss, then Meknes.
  •  Morning in Meknes, city tour of Rabat, arrive in Casablanca for dinner.
  •  Casablanca city tour including Hassan II Mosque, then return to Marrakech.
  • Departure day with luggage storage option and last-minute city exploration.​

At 6:00 p.m., join us for a charming Caleche ride through the city, taking you to the lively Jemaa el-Fnaa Square, which will be buzzing with vibrant energy and local culture at this hour. At 08:15 a.m., we will begin our guided city tour of Marrakech, a vibrant city nestled among palm groves and surrounded by iconic ochre-colored walls. We’ll explore the city's historic highlights, including the bustling Jemaa el-Fnaa Square, the lively Medina with its colorful souks, and the majestic Koutoubia Mosque. En route, we’ll stop in a charming mountain village for a brief break. At 07:15 a.m., we depart Boumalne Dades and travel through the scenic Valley of the Kasbahs, known for its historic adobe fortresses nestled between the High and Saharan Atlas. Along the way, we’ll admire lush oases, palm groves, and charming villages. By 09:00 a.m., we arrive at Todgha Gorge to explore its towering canyon walls—one of Morocco’s most breathtaking natural wonders. Optional dune excursion in Merzouga offers sunset views and dinner in the desert.

As Morocco’s cultural heart and home to the world’s oldest university, Fez boasts a fascinating Medina that captures authentic Arab heritage. Lunch will be served in a historic palace. At 3:00 p.m., we depart for Volubilis, a remarkable Roman archaeological site (entry included). At 5:45 p.m., we reach the sacred city of Moulay Idriss. After free time, we continue to Meknes, arriving at 7:30 p.m. for hotel check-in and a delightful dinner.

At 8:30 a.m., after breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will lead a guided tour of Meknes, a UNESCO World Heritage Site founded in the 11th century by the Almoravids. Once the capital under Sultan Muley Ismail, it boasts grand Spanish-Moorish architecture and towering gates like Bab el Mansur. We’ll visit El Hedim Square and the Mausoleum of Moulay Ismail (entry included), with free time to explore. At 12:30 p.m., we depart, stopping for lunch en route. This morning, join our Travelxploria representative for a guided tour of Casablanca, Morocco’s largest city and economic hub. We’ll explore Mohamed V Square, surrounded by grand Moorish architecture and vibrant fountains, and stroll through the palm-lined Arab League Park. You’ll also visit the charming Medina and enjoy entry to the stunning seaside Hassan II Mosque, famed for its 210-meter-high minaret with a laser beam pointing toward Mecca. After the tour, enjoy free time for lunch in the scenic La Corniche area. At 3:30 p.m., we depart for Marrakech, arriving by 6:45 p.m. Dinner and overnight stay at the hotel included.

packages.overview:
• ​Marrakech: Arrival in Marrakech, transfer to hotel, and evening Caleche ride to the vibrant Jemaa el-Fnaa Square.
  • Marrakech: City tour exploring the Medina, Koutoubia Mosque, and Bahia Palace, with optional Fantasy dinner show in the evening.
  •  Marrakech to Boumalne Dades: Scenic drive through the Atlas Mountains with visits to Ait Benhaddou and Ouarzazate, ending in Boumalne Dades.
  • Boumalne Dades to Erfoud: Journey through Todgha Gorge and Khorbat, with optional desert excursion to Merzouga at sunset.
  • Erfoud to Fez: Cross the Atlas Mountains via Midelt, Azrou, and Bhalil before reaching the spiritual city of Fez.
  • Fez to Tangier: Guided tour of Fez's historic sites and souks, followed by travel through Rif valleys to Tangier.
  • Tangier to Rabat via Asilah: Free time in Tangier, lunch stop in the coastal town of Asilah, and arrival in Rabat.
  • Rabat to Marrakech via Casablanca: Panoramic tour of Rabat and Casablanca, including the Hassan II Mosque, before returning to Marrakech.
  • Departure from Marrakech: Tour concludes after breakfast with flexible luggage storage and optional late check-out.

packages.overview:
• ​Marrakech: Arrival in Marrakech, hotel transfer, and evening Caleche ride to lively Jemaa el-Fnaa Square.
  • Marrakech: Guided city tour of Marrakech’s historic sites including Bahia Palace and Koutoubia Mosque; afternoon free time.
  • Marrakech – Ouzoud – Fez: Scenic journey to Ouzoud Falls for lunch and boat ride, then travel to Fez via the Atlas Mountains.
  • Fez – Tangier: Explore Fez’s rich heritage with a local guide and enjoy a traditional lunch before heading to Tangier.
  • Tangier – Asilah – Rabat: Morning in Tangier, coastal visit to Asilah, and evening arrival in Rabat with dinner included.
  • Rabat – Casablanca – Marrakech: City tour of Rabat and Casablanca’s Hassan II Mosque, then return to Marrakech for dinner.
  •  Marrakech (Departure): End of the journey with free time; check-out and luggage storage available for your convenience.
